description: Trigger and behavior cases for contract verification design: schema/shape validation, provider/consumer roles, versioning and backward-compat gates, error envelopes, no UI.

cases.prompt: Partners keep breaking when we release API changes. Design contract tests that catch breaking changes before they ship.

assertions: Pins a machine-readable contract source of truth per surface (or flags its extraction as the first deliverable).
Designs provider-side schema validation including error envelopes, plus a CI compatibility gate with explicit additive-pass/breaking-fail rules.
Covers every version in its support window and ties breaking changes to the versioning/deprecation policy from api-event-architect.
Stays UI-free and hands implementation off.

cases.prompt: Our integration tests fake the payment provider, and last month the fake drifted from the real API. Prevent that.

assertions: Designs consumer-side contract tests: fakes/recordings re-validated against the provider schema or sandbox on a schedule.
Adds tolerance tests for documented variation (absent optionals, unknown fields, error shapes).
Links each faked seam in the integration boundary map to the contract artifact that keeps it honest.

cases.prompt: Design our public API's versioning and deprecation policy and the webhook envelope format.

assertions: Versioning policy and webhook envelope DESIGN is the shipped api-event-architect; this skill verifies implementations against that design.

cases.prompt: Write contract tests for the orders API — note the implementation currently returns snake_case but the published docs say camelCase.

assertions: Refuses to silently pin tests to the implementation OR the docs while they disagree.
Routes the discrepancy to source-of-truth-reconciler (fix implementation vs re-version the contract) before locking the verification target.
Documents the conflict explicitly in the output.
